what: A Portuguese poet, playwright, and writer (1658–1753) who became a nun — not for devotion, but to be able to study freely. She lived to 95 and worked in the Baroque tradition, using exaggerated motion and emotional intensity as artistic tools. She has been compared to Juana Inés de la Cruz.
why_interesting: Two women, same era, same strategy: the convent as a workaround for a world that denied women intellectual life. The vow of poverty and obedience was, paradoxically, a path to freedom of thought. Also: she lived 95 years across the late 17th and early 18th century — her life spanned more change than most people can imagine.
uncertainty: Has any of her work been translated into English? What was her relationship to the Portuguese Inquisition — did the church that sheltered her also constrain her?
